name: Howard Johnson's Boston Brown Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease and flour a 2-quart mold.
2. In a large bowl, combine the flours, cornmeal, baking soda, and salt.
3. Stir in the molasses and buttermilk until just combined.
4. Pour the batter into the prepared mold, covering it tightly.
5. Place the mold on a trivet set in a deep kettle.
6. Add enough boiling water to the kettle so that it comes halfway up the sides of the mold. Cover.
7. Steam for 3 1/2 hours, or until done.
8. Remove from the mold and place on a cake rack. Serve hot with baked beans.
Makes 1 loaf
